paradigm: Mk.XII Phase 3a gemma family-internal scale closure — alternative path 1 (int4 pre-quantized) hypothesis-confirmation dispatch
task_axis: alternative path 1 (int8/int4 fallback) per omega-blocker.alternative_paths[0]; H1 (mfs throughput cap) hypothesis test
diagnostic_framing: HYPOTHESIS-CONFIRMATION (own 4 + omega-blocker mandatory_commit_gate compliant) — NOT blind retry of fp16
pod_region: EUR-NO-2 (DE) — DIFFERENT from v1/v2/v3 (us-ne-1); incidentally tests H3 region hypothesis

quantization_variant.raw_10_protocol_consistency_with_2b_9b_f: SEPARATE_AXIS — int4 quantization is a different regime; not directly comparable to 2b/9b fp16 cells

error_chain: torch.cuda.is_available() = False (despite nvidia-smi showing H100 + CUDA Driver 580.126.09 / CUDA 13.0)
bitsandbytes 0.43.3 reports 'compiled without GPU support' (consequence of cuInit failure, not bnb mis-build)
transformers quantizer_bnb_4bit.validate_environment raised RuntimeError('No GPU found. A GPU is needed for quantization.')
stage_2_model_load.root_cause_diagnosis: RunPod pod template runpod/pytorch:2.4.0-py3.11-cuda12.4.1-devel-ubuntu22.04 has stale CUDA driver state at container startup; nvidia-uvm module/device permissions race; classic cuInit=999 RunPod pod-cold-start issue. NOT a gemma/quantization/anima-protocol issue.
stage_2_model_load.fix_for_future_attempt: (a) call nvidia-smi BEFORE python imports torch (warm cuda driver state), (b) restart pod once if cuInit=999 on fresh pod, (c) try newer pytorch image (runpod/pytorch:2.5.x or 2.8.x), (d) test on a different host (cold-start fault is host-specific in RunPod EUR-NO-2)

stage_3_cmt_measurement.reason: stage 2 load failed before measurement could begin

h1_mfs_throughput_cap_test_result.evidence: 15.82GB downloaded in 293.7s sustained (54 MB/s avg) with NO silent termination, NO platform interference, NO xet disable issue, NO process kill. The same pod template that failed at 40-90GB on fp16 sustained 16GB cleanly. Throughput cap WOULD have triggered termination at the same MB/s threshold regardless of total size; it did not.
h1_mfs_throughput_cap_test_result.implication: Platform fault on fp16 27B (3 prior attempts) is NOT throughput-bound. Size-bound (H2 RunPod ingress quota) is now the leading hypothesis — 27B fp16 (54GB) crosses a quota threshold that 4bit (16GB) does not.
h1_mfs_throughput_cap_test_result.caveat_strength: PARTIAL: download phase passed cleanly, but subsequent stage 2 (model load) failed for an ORTHOGONAL reason (cuda driver init), not platform termination. So 'full int4 measurement PASS' is not achieved; 'download phase H1 test' is conclusive.

h2_ingress_quota_hypothesis_now_priority.rationale: (1) v1 fp16 crash at ~59GB cache (75% of 54GB), (2) v2 fp16 crash at ~94GB total observed bytes (xet 51-61 concurrent connections, total bytes >> file size due to xet de-dup overhead), (3) v3 fp16 crash relauch at 12% (4/34 files = ~6GB) — possibly accumulated from v2/v3 same-region pod allocation + ingress credit consumed, (4) int4 download 16GB clean. Pattern: >=40-50GB ingress per pod-lifetime triggers platform termination; <20GB safe.
h2_ingress_quota_hypothesis_now_priority.test_design: alternative path 2 (persistent network volume) — pre-download once on a long-lived volume, then attach to short-lived measurement pod. Eliminates per-pod ingress charge for the fp16 weights.
h2_ingress_quota_hypothesis_now_priority.alternative_test_design: alternative path 4 (offline rsync) — upload fp16 weights from local Mac to pod via SSH (egress to pod from Mac counts differently); slower but bypasses HF Hub to RunPod-network ingress quota.

HMK-A_GEMMA_SCALE_INVARIANT.rationale: 27b cell still missing — int4 measurement did not complete CMT stage. Even if int4 cell had completed, raw#10 caveat: int4 vs fp16 measurement regime difference would prevent direct apples-to-apples 3-point pairwise BBA evaluation against 2b/9b fp16.

HMK-B_GEMMA_SCALE_BREAK.rationale: 27b cell still missing — cannot detect scale break.

HQS-C_FALSIFICATION_MIXED_BACKBONE_CONDI.rationale: 27b cell still missing — anchored to axis 109 Qwen MIXED_BACKBONE_CONDITIONAL CONFIRMED.

raw_10_caveats: 1. int4 quantization regime != fp16 — even if int4 cell had captured, comparing it against 2b/9b fp16 would mix two measurement protocols (raw#12 frozen-spec compliance violation); mitigation would require re-measuring 2b/9b in int4 too.

2. unsloth/gemma-2-27b-bnb-4bit is BASE variant (matches 2b/9b base baseline); -it instruct shift caveat does NOT apply here — protocol consistency on base/instruct axis is preserved (this is one of two axes; quantization axis is the violator).

3. Pod region was EUR-NO-2 (DE), NOT us-ne-1 like v1/v2/v3. Side-effect: incidentally tests H3 (region hypothesis). Download succeeded in DE, but this could be DE-specific OR int4-specific OR both — not disambiguated.

4. Pod terminated mid-diagnosis by SELF-INFLICTED modprobe call (helper attempted to reload nvidia_uvm to fix cuInit=999) — runpod treats kernel module manipulation as abuse trigger, auto-killed the pod. NOT a platform-fault termination; was operator error during diagnostic phase. Cost burn $0.60 — diagnostic cost, not measurement cost.

5. cuInit=999 on fresh runpod/pytorch:2.4.0-cuda12.4.1 is a known recurring RunPod pod-cold-start fault (host driver 580.126.09/CUDA 13.0 vs container CUDA 12.4 mismatch tolerance issue). Should be added to anima_runpod_orchestrator pre-flight check (own 4 step (d) canonicalization).

6. H1 mfs-throughput-cap rejection is based on download phase succeeding once (16GB) vs fp16 failing 3 times (54GB, >=40GB ingress) — strong but single-data-point. Re-running int4 dispatch with cuda fix would corroborate (and would attempt the int4 measurement which is INFORMATIVE for raw#10 separate-axis scale curve).

omega_blocker_status_update_recommendati.field_update: remaining_hypotheses[0] (H1 mfs throughput cap) DEPRIORITIZED to 'rejected_via_alt_path_1_download_phase'; remaining_hypotheses[1] (H2 ingress quota) PROMOTED to priority 1; alternative_paths[1] (persistent_network_volume) becomes the canonical next attempt path; alternative_paths[0] (int8_fallback) status: PARTIAL_PASS_DOWNLOAD_PHASE_ONLY — re-dispatch with cuda fix is INFORMATIVE but will not produce cell directly comparable to 2b/9b fp16 (raw#10 separate-axis caveat).
omega_blocker_status_update_recommendati.decision_criteria_for_resolution_unchang: still requires successful gemma-2-27b fp16 measurement OR Llama family-internal alternative OR pre-register protocol revision with new gemma-2-27b-free path

next_cycle_recommendations: (priority 1) alternative path 2: provision RunPod persistent network volume (~$8/month for 60GB), pre-download gemma-2-27b fp16 weights ONCE on a dedicated download pod with cuInit fix, attach volume to measurement pod; survives pod cycles. Directly tests H2 ingress quota.

(priority 1.5, low-risk) re-dispatch alternative path 1 (int4) on a fresh pod with cuda fix (nvidia-smi-before-import + fallback pod restart on cuInit=999); if it passes, captures int4 27b cell as SEPARATE-axis evidence (raw#10 caveat). If 2b/9b are also re-run in int4, full int4 family-internal scale curve becomes available as a parallel-axis dataset.
(priority 2) own 4 step (d) canonicalization: add cuInit=999 pre-flight check + auto-restart-once policy to tool/anima_runpod_orchestrator.hexa large-model-load template.
(priority 3) alternative path 5 (Llama family-internal) remains a dependency-blocked option (task #6 manual HF unblock).
pod_termination_confirmation: Pod 83dlyisq3tlbef no longer exists in runpodctl pod list (verified via 'pod not found 404' on pod get); deleted at ~13:50Z after self-inflicted modprobe call during cuInit=999 diagnosis.
